title: Extraction of high molecular weight insect DNA for long-read sequencing
author: Berenice J. Lafon
materials: If the DNA forms a pellet:
Gently mix by inverting the tube.
If a DNA pellet is not present:
If the DNA pellet does not dissolve completely (presence of fat or protein), incubate at 37°C for 30 minutes with minimal agitation (Thermomixer).
Leave it overnight at room temperature.

steps.title: Depending on how the specimen has...
steps.instruction: Depending on how the specimen has been sampled and preserved, one may adapt the DNA extractions as follows:

steps.title: If the tissue sample was preserved...
steps.instruction: If the tissue sample was preserved in RNAlater, pour the contents of the tube into a Petri dish. Blot the RNAlater onto a sterile pad, then place the tissue pieces into a 1.5 mL tube with 1 mL of PBS to wash out the RNAlater. Blot again on a sterile pad before placing the tissue in the mortar.

steps.title: If the specimen was preserved in...
steps.instruction: If the specimen was preserved in ethanol, pour the contents of the tube into a Petri dish. Before placing the tissue in the mortar, lightly blot it with a sterile pad.

steps.title: If the specimen is alive or...
steps.instruction: If the specimen is alive or has been killed and stored at -80°C, dissect it in absolute ethanol in a Petri dish. Lightly blot the tissue and cut it into small pieces on a sterile compress before placing it in the mortar.

steps.title: Select tissues for extraction (avoid cuticle,...
steps.instruction: Select tissues for extraction (avoid cuticle, bristles, gut, defensive glands, eggs...). Give priority to muscles and respiratory system (tracheae). Pictured is a beetle specimen of Carabus hispanus for which we extracted high molecular weight insect DNA for long-read sequencing.

steps.title: Place 500 μL lysis buffer (ATL...
steps.instruction: Place 500 μL lysis buffer (ATL from the Qiagen DNeasy Blood & Tissue Kit containing EDTA and SDS) in a mortar.

steps.title: Grind with a pestle for a...
steps.instruction: Grind with a pestle for a few minutes until the tissue is well crushed.

steps.title: Recover all the crushed tissue with a P1000 pipette
steps.instruction: Recover all the crushed tissue with a P1000 pipette. If the grind is not sufficiently fluid, cut off the tip of the P1000 cone to facilitate collection. Transfer to a 2 mL tube.

steps.title: Add Proteinase K (included in the...
steps.instruction: Add Proteinase K (included in the Qiagen DNeasy Blood & Tissue Kit ) at 10% of the buffer volume (e.g. 50 µL Proteinase K to 500 µL buffer ).

steps.title: Depending on the amount of tissue,...
steps.instruction: Depending on the amount of tissue, adjust the volume by adding lysis buffer to completely submerge the tissue.

steps.title: Gently mix by swirling the tube (do not vortex)
steps.instruction: Gently mix by swirling the tube (do not vortex).

steps.title: Add RNase A to digest the RNA
steps.instruction: Add RNase A to digest the RNA. Qiagen recommends 4 μL RNase A for 200 μL (buffer + Proteinase K), so adjust the volume of RNase A accordingly (e.g. add 11 μL RNase A for 550 μL volume).

steps.title: Mix by gentle swirling of the tube (do not vortex)
steps.instruction: Mix by gentle swirling of the tube (do not vortex). Centrifuge for a maximum of 3 seconds.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 11,000 g for 10...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 11,000 g for 10 minutes at room temperature to pellet the remaining tissue.

steps.title: Collect the supernatant, avoiding any floating...
steps.instruction: Collect the supernatant, avoiding any floating fat layer, and transfer to a fresh 2 mL tube. Tip: Pipet in 100 µL steps to determine the final volume of supernatant (e.g. you hope to pipet between 450 and 500 µL).

steps.title: Incubate at 56°C (in a Eppendorf ThermoMixer F1
steps.instruction: Incubate at 56°C (in a Eppendorf ThermoMixer F1.5 at 300 rpm; agitation is not mandatory) for 1 to 2 hours, depending on the progress of the digestion.

steps.title: Important for subsequent steps
steps.instruction: Important for subsequent steps: If the volume of supernatant obtained exceeds 600 μL, separate this volume into two tubes.

steps.title: Add sodium acetate (3 M) to...
steps.instruction: Add sodium acetate (3 M) to 10% of the supernatant volume (e.g. 50 μL for 500 μL supernatant).

steps.title: Mix gently by swirling the tube (do not vortex)
steps.instruction: Mix gently by swirling the tube (do not vortex).

steps.title: Add absolute ethanol equal to twice...
steps.instruction: Add absolute ethanol equal to twice the volume of the supernatant (e.g. 1,000 μL ethanol for 500 μL supernatant).

steps.title: Mix gently by inversion of the...
steps.instruction: Mix gently by inversion of the tube until the DNA pellet forms ( approximately 20 inversions ).

steps.title: Collect it and transfer it to a 1
steps.instruction: Collect it and transfer it to a 1.5 mL tube containing 1 mL of 70% ethanol . Tip: Collect the DNA pellet by aspiration with a P1000 cone, taking care not to aspirate the pellet completely so as not to break it. Otherwise, remove the supernatant by gentle pipetting without aspirating the pellet.

steps.title: Repeat the previous step a second...
steps.instruction: Repeat the previous step a second time to make two washes with 70% ethanol.

steps.title: Remove the 70% ethanol by pipetting...
steps.instruction: Remove the 70% ethanol by pipetting without aspirating the DNA pellet.

steps.title: Centrifuge for a maximum of 3 seconds
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for a maximum of 3 seconds.

steps.title: Remove the remaining ethanol with a P10 pipette
steps.instruction: Remove the remaining ethanol with a P10 pipette.

steps.title: Allow to dry for 5 minutes...
steps.instruction: Allow to dry for 5 minutes with the tube open to allow the 70% ethanol to evaporate.

steps.title: Centrifuge the tube at 11,000 g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tube at 11,000 g for 10 minutes to pellet.

steps.title: Pipette off the supernatant
steps.instruction: Pipette off the supernatant.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of 70% ethanol...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of 70% ethanol to wash the DNA pellet. Tap the tube to dislodge and resuspend.

steps.title: Remove the 70% ethanol and repeat...
steps.instruction: Remove the 70% ethanol and repeat the previous step to perform two washes.

steps.title: Remove the ethanol by pipetting without aspirating the DNA pellet
steps.instruction: Remove the ethanol by pipetting without aspirating the DNA pellet.

steps.title: Centrifuge for a maximum of 3 seconds
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for a maximum of 3 seconds.

steps.title: Remove the remaining ethanol with a P10 pipette
steps.instruction: Remove the remaining ethanol with a P10 pipette.

steps.title: Resuspend the DNA pellet in water...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the DNA pellet in water (between 100 μL and 200 μL , depending on the size of the pellet) to allow it to settle properly.

steps.title: The next day, if impurities remain,...
steps.instruction: The next day, if impurities remain, centrifuge for 5 minutes at 11,000 g and collect the supernatant. If any fat remains, pipette the supernatant, taking care not to collect the fat, and transfer to a new 1.5 mL tube.

steps.title: Perform a Qubit BR DNA assay
steps.instruction: Perform a Qubit BR DNA assay.

steps.title: Load on a 1% agarose gel
steps.instruction: Load on a 1% agarose gel. A large band above 10 kb should normally be observed. Degraded RNAs (bands below 200 bp) are often left over for purification during library construction.

steps.title: Purification with AMPure 1X beads
steps.instruction: Purification with AMPure 1X beads. Make a Qubit BR and Nanodrop assays. Pictured is an agarose gel showing DNA after extraction (left) and DNA after purification with beads (right).

steps.title: Video showing the selection of tissues...
steps.instruction: Video showing the selection of tissues as in the case 2.1 . This is a specimen of Carabus hispanus that was freshly collected and dissected in absolute ethanol. Here we see the selection of internal tissues (mostly muscles) after the specimen has been cut into two parts. The tissues are lightly blotted before being placed in the mortar.

steps.title: Video showing the formation of the...
steps.instruction: Video showing the formation of the DNA pellet as expected from a high molecular weight insect DNA.
